title: Benchmark harness + baseline timings
goal: Ship a reproducible bench harness that measures AXIOM-Compute kernels end-to-end on CPU (reference) and Lavapipe (Vulkan software), records JSON baselines in .pipeline/benchmarks/, and establishes the scaffolding for future real-GPU + llama.cpp-Vulkan comparisons. Without this, the DESIGN.md §5 kill criteria are unprovable.
scope_in: Criterion-based microbench crate in `benches/` at workspace level (or axc-driver) timing: (a) source→SPIR-V compile time, (b) Vulkan dispatch time for saxpy + vector_add on Lavapipe, (c) CPU reference equivalent (Rust loop) for same N
Bench run via `cargo bench` at the workspace or crate level
Baseline JSON: `.pipeline/benchmarks/baselines.json` containing per-kernel per-machine metrics (compile_ns, dispatch_ns_1k/1m, cpu_ns_1m, compile_size_bytes) with timestamp and git SHA
Regression gate: new `axc bench` CLI subcommand (or standalone integration test) that runs the bench suite and fails if any metric regresses > 15% vs checked-in baseline (configurable threshold)
Two benchmarked kernels in scope: saxpy (1024-element + 1M-element), vector_add (1024 + 1M)
CPU reference: identical math in a Rust function, timed separately via criterion
Correctness check: each bench iteration also runs a CPU reference on the same inputs and panics if GPU output diverges > 1e-6 (piggyback on existing M1.5 correctness machinery)
Machine metadata recorded: ICD name (Lavapipe vs vendor), device name (llvmpipe vs RTX/RDNA/Arc), OS, rustc version, axiom-compute git SHA
Reproducibility: `cargo bench` in a fresh clone should produce a baseline JSON with the same STRUCTURE (values vary by hardware); run order deterministic, warmup configurable
Documentation: `BENCHMARKS.md` at repo root explaining how to run, read, and interpret results — and a MILESTONE-EXIT matrix showing which baseline metrics correspond to which kill criteria
scope_out: llama.cpp Vulkan comparison baselines (M2.5 — depends on Q4_K_M kernel)
Real tensor-core dispatch timing (depends on CI hardware)
Cross-machine perf regression tracking (needs DB / CI harness)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace: 471 baseline preserved, +N new tests from bench-correctness gate
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (bench crate included)
cargo bench --workspace completes in < 120s on Lavapipe with default warmup + sample count
baselines.json contains at least 4 entries (saxpy_1k, saxpy_1m, vector_add_1k, vector_add_1m) with all required metadata fields present
Regression gate: intentionally slowing a kernel by inserting a dummy for-loop (in a test fixture) makes the regression test fail with > 15% slowdown detected
BENCHMARKS.md present at repo root and gittracked
All GPU bench iterations panic on correctness divergence (not silent)
Machine metadata includes all 6 required fields
open_questions_for_architect: Where does the bench crate live: workspace-level `benches/` or a new `axc-bench` crate? Workspace-level is simpler; separate crate is more modular. Recommend workspace-level `benches/` inside axc-driver for simplicity.
Criterion settings: default 100 samples + 3s measurement + 3s warmup sufficient? For sub-microsecond dispatches (tiny N), may be too coarse. Custom profiling config per kernel?
CPU reference: run on same thread or spawn new one? Same thread is simpler. Criterion handles measurement.
Baseline update workflow: auto-update on CI main branch, or manual `axc bench --bless` command? Recommend manual blessing to avoid baseline drift from noise.
Regression threshold: 15% default, configurable per kernel in baselines.json? Recommend uniform 15% for simplicity.
Machine metadata: how is ICD name captured? Query VulkanContext's physical device name + driver version. Recommend storing the device name string verbatim.
Bench correctness check: if a kernel diverges by 1e-6, criterion discards that sample but continues? Or panics? Panic is safer — silent perf of wrong output is worse than loud failure.
Reproducibility fixture: deterministic input data (fixed seed, known values) vs random? Use fixed-seed PRNG (StdRng::seed_from_u64(42)) for reproducibility.
